volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Andromache (Section 185 - Section 335)

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: τὸ μὴ δίκαιον ὅστις ἀνθρώπων ἔχει .
sections.translation: in every case , that is , where a man embraces injustice in his early days . Now I am afraid that my being a slave will prevent thee listening to me in spite of many a just plea , or if I win my case , I fear I may be damaged on this very ground , for the high and mighty cannot brook refuting arguments

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: πικρῶς φέρουσι τῶν ἐλασσόνων ὕπο ·
sections.translation: from their inferiors ; still I will not be convicted of betraying my own cause . Tell me , proud young wife , what assurance can make me confident of wresting from thee thy lawful lord ? Is it that Laconias capital yields to Phrygia ?

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: τύχῃ θ ’ ὑπερθεῖ , κἄμ ’ ἐλευθέραν ὁρᾷς ;
sections.translation: is it that my fortune outstrips thine ? or that in me thou seest a free woman ? Am I so elated by my youth , my full healthy figure , the extent of my city , the number of my friends that I wish to supplant thee in thy home ? Is my purpose to take thy place and rear myself a race

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: δούλους ἐμαυτῇ τ ’ ἀθλίαν ἐφολκίδα ;
sections.translation: of slaves , mere appendages to my misery ? or , supposing thou bear no children , will any one endure that sons of mine should rule oer Phthia ? Ah no ! there is the love that Hellas bears me , both for Hectors sake and for my own humble rank forsooth , that never knew a queens estate in Troy .

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: οὐκ ἐξ ἐμῶν σε φαρμάκων στυγεῖ πόσις ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis not my sorcery that makes thy husband hate thee , nay , but thy own failure to prove thyself his help-meet . [*] Herein lies love ’ s only charm tis not beauty , lady , but virtuous acts that win our husbands ’ hearts . And though it gall thee to be told so , albeit thy city in Laconia

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: μέγ ’ ἐστί , τὴν δὲ Σκῦρον οὐδαμοῦ τίθης ·
sections.translation: is no doubt a mighty fact , yet thou findest no place for his Scyros , displaying wealth ’ midst poverty and setting Menelaus above Achilles : and that is what alienates thy lord . Take heed ; for a woman , though bestowed upon a worthless husband , must be with him content , and ne ’ er advance presumptuous claims .

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: εἰ δ ’ ἀμφὶ Θρῄκην χιόνι τὴν κατάρρυτον
sections.translation: Suppose thou hadst wedded a prince of Thrace , the land of flood and melting snow , where one lord shares his affections with a host of wives , wouldst thou have slain them ? If so , thou wouldst have set a stigma of insatiate lust on all our sex .

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: αἰσχρόν γε · καίτοι χείρον ’ ἀρσένων νόσον
sections.translation: A shameful charge ! And yet herein we suffer more than men , though we make a good stand against it . Ah ! my dear lord Hector , for thy sake would I e ’ en brook a rival , if ever Cypris led thee astray , and oft in days gone by I held thy bastard babes to my own breast ,

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: ἐπέσχον , ἵνα σοι μηδὲν ἐνδοίην πικρόν .
sections.translation: to spare thee any cause for grief . By this course I bound my husband to me by virtue ’ s chains , whereas thou wilt never so much as let the drops of dew from heaven above settle on thy lord , in thy jealous fear . [*]

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: ζήτει παρελθεῖν · τῶν κακῶν γὰρ μητέρων
sections.translation: Oh ! seek not to surpass thy mother in hankering after men , for ’ tis well that all wise children should avoid the habits of such evil mothers .

sections.label: Section 232
sections.original: δέσποιν ’, ὅσον σοι ῥᾳδίως προσίσταται ,
sections.translation: Mistress mine , be persuaded to come to terms with her , as far as readily comes within thy power .

sections.label: Section 234
sections.original: τί σεμνομυθεῖς κεἰς ἀγῶν ’ ἔρχῃ λόγων ,
sections.translation: Why this haughty tone , this bandying of words , as if , forsooth , thou , not I , wert the virtuous wife ?

sections.label: Section 236
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐφ ’ οἷς γε νῦν καθέστηκας λόγοις .
sections.translation: Thy present claims at any rate give thee small title thereto .

sections.label: Section 237
sections.original: ὁ νοῦς ὁ σός μοι μὴ ξυνοικοίη , γύναι .
sections.translation: Woman , may my bosom never harbour such ideas as thine !

sections.label: Section 238
sections.original: νέα πέφυκας καὶ λέγεις αἰσχρῶν πέρι .
sections.translation: Thou art young to speak on so delicate a subject .

sections.label: Section 239
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ οὐ λέγεις γε , δρᾷς δέ μ ’ εἰς ὅσον δύνῃ .
sections.translation: As for thee , thou dost not speak thereof but , as thou canst , dost put it into action against me .

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: οὐκ αὖ σιωπῇ Κύπριδος ἀλγήσεις πέρι ;
sections.translation: Canst thou not conceal thy pangs of jealousy ?

sections.label: Section 241
sections.original: τί δ ’; οὐ γυναιξὶ ταῦτα πρῶτα πανταχοῦ ;
sections.translation: What ! doth not every woman put this first of all ?

sections.label: Section 242
sections.original: ναί ,
sections.translation: Yes , if her experiences are happy ; otherwise , there is no honour in speaking of them .

sections.label: Section 243
sections.original: οὐ βαρβάρων νόμοισιν οἰκοῦμεν πόλιν .
sections.translation: Barbarians ’ laws are not a standard for our city .

sections.label: Section 244
sections.original: κἀκεῖ τά γ ’ αἰσχρὰ κἀνθάδ ’ αἰσχύνην ἔχει .
sections.translation: Alike in Asia and in Hellas infamy attends base actions .

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: σοφὴ σοφὴ σύ · κατθανεῖν δ ’ ὅμως σε δεῖ .
sections.translation: Clever , clever quibbler ! yet die thou must and shalt .

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ἄγαλμα Θέτιδος εἰς σ ’ ἀποβλέπον ;
sections.translation: Dost see the image of Thetis with her eye upon thee ?

sections.label: Section 247
sections.original: μισοῦν γε πατρίδα σὴν Ἀχιλλέως φόνῳ .
sections.translation: A bitter foe to thy country because of the death of Achilles .

sections.label: Section 248
sections.original: Ἑλένη νιν ὤλεσ ’, οὐκ ἐγώ , μήτηρ δὲ σή .
sections.translation: ’ Twas not I that slew him , but Helen that mother of thine .

sections.label: Section 249
sections.original: ἦ καὶ πρόσω γὰρ τῶν ἐμῶν ψαύσεις κακῶν ;
sections.translation: Pray , is it thy intention to probe my wounds yet deeper ?

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: ἰδοὺ σιωπῶ κἀπιλάζυμαι στόμα .
sections.translation: Behold , I am dumb , my lips are closed .

sections.label: Section 251
sections.original: ἐκεῖνο λέξον , οὗπερ εἵνεκ ’ ἐστάλην .
sections.translation: Tell me that which was my only reason for coming hither .

sections.label: Section 252
sections.original: λέγω σ ’ ἐγὼ νοῦν οὐκ ἔχειν ὅσον σε δεῖ .
sections.translation: No ! all I tell thee is , thou hast less wisdom than thou needest .

sections.label: Section 253
sections.original: λείψεις τόδ ’ ἁγνὸν τέμενος ἐναλίας θεοῦ ;
sections.translation: Wilt thou leave these hallowed precincts of the sea-goddess ?

sections.label: Section 254
sections.original: εἰ μὴ θανοῦμαί γ ’· εἰ δὲ μή , οὐ λείψω ποτέ .
sections.translation: Yes , if I am not to die for it ; otherwise , I never will .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: ὡς τοῦτ ’ ἄραρε , κοὐ μενῶ πόσιν μολεῖν .
sections.translation: Since that is thy resolve , I shall not even wait my lord ’ s return .

sections.label: Section 256
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ οὐδ ’ ἐγὼ μὴν πρόσθεν ἐκδώσω μέ σοι .
sections.translation: Nor yet will I , at any rate ere that , surrender to thee .

sections.label: Section 257
sections.original: πῦρ σοι προσοίσω , κοὐ τὸ σὸν προσκέψομαι —
sections.translation: I will bring fire to bear on thee , and pay no heed to thy entreaties .

sections.label: Section 258
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ οὖν κάταιθε · θεοὶ γὰρ εἴσονται τάδε .
sections.translation: Kindle thy blaze then ; the gods will witness it .

sections.label: Section 259
sections.original: καὶ χρωτὶ δεινῶν τραυμάτων ἀλγηδόνας .
sections.translation: And make thy flesh to writhe by cruel wounds .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: σφάζ ’, αἱμάτου θεᾶς βωμόν , ἣ μέτεισί σε .
sections.translation: Begin thy butchery , stain the altar of the goddess with blood , for she will visit thy iniquity .

sections.label: Section 261
sections.original: ὦ βάρβαρον σὺ θρέμμα καὶ σκληρὸν θράσος ,
sections.translation: Barbarian creature , hardened in impudence , wilt thou brave death itself ? Still will I find speedy means to make thee quit this seat of thy free-will ; such a bait have I to lure thee with . But I will hide my meaning ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: κρύψω , τὸ δ ’ ἔργον αὐτὸ σημανεῖ τάχα .
sections.translation: which the event itself shall soon declare . Yes , keep thy seat , for I will make thee rise , though molten lead is holding thee there , before Achilles ’ son , thy trusted champion , arrive . Exit Hermione .

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: πέποιθα . δεινὸν δ ’ ἑρπετῶν μὲν ἀγρίων
sections.translation: My trusted champion , yes ! how strange it is , that , though some god hath devised cures for mortals against the venom of reptiles , no man ever yet hath discovered aught to cure a woman ’ s venom , which is far worse than viper ’ s sting or scorching flame ; so terrible a curse are we to mankind .

sections.label: Section 1.274
sections.original: Ἦ μεγάλων ἀχέων ἄρ ’ ὑπῆρξεν , ὅτ ’ Ἰδαίαν
sections.translation: Ah ! what sorrows did the son of Zeus and Maia herald ,

sections.label: Section 1.275
sections.original: ἐς νάπαν ἦλθ ’ ὁ Μαί-
sections.translation: in the day he came to Ida ’ s glen , guiding that fair young trio of goddesses , all girded for the fray in bitter rivalry about their beauty ,

sections.label: Section 1.280
sections.original: σταθμοὺς ἐπὶ βούτα ,
sections.translation: to the shepherd ’ s fold , where dwelt the youthful herdsman all alone by the hearth of his lonely hut .

sections.label: Section 1.284
sections.original: ταὶ δ ’ ἐπεὶ ὑλόκομον νάπος ἤλυθον οὐρειᾶν
sections.translation: Soon as they reached the wooded glen , in gushing mountain

sections.label: Section 1.285
sections.original: πιδάκων νίψαν αἰ-
sections.translation: springs they bathed [*] their dazzling skin , then sought the son of Priam , comparing their rival charms in more than rancorous phrase . [*] But Cypris won the day by her deceitful promises ,

sections.label: Section 1.290
sections.original: τερπνοῖς μὲν ἀκοῦσαι ,
sections.translation: sweet-sounding words , but fraught with ruthless overthrow to Phrygia ’ s hapless town and Ilium ’ s towers .

sections.label: Section 2.293
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ εἴθ ’ ὑπὲρ κεφαλὰν ἔβαλεν κακὸν
sections.translation: Would God his mother had smitten him a cruel death-blow [*] on the head before he made his home on Ida ’ s

sections.label: Section 2.295
sections.original: ον κατοικίσαι λέπας ·
sections.translation: slopes , in the hour Cassandra , standing by the holy bay-tree , cried out , Slay him , for he will bring most grievous bane on Priam ’ s town . To every prince she went , to every elder sued

sections.label: Section 2.300
sections.original: των βρέφος φονεύειν ;
sections.translation: for the babe ’ s destruction .

sections.label: Section 2.301
sections.original: οὔτ ’ ἂν ἐπ ’ Ἰλιάσι ζυγὸν ἤλυθε
sections.translation: Ah ! had they listened , Ilium ’ s daughters ne ’ er had felt the yoke of slavery , and [*] thou , lady , hadst been established in the royal palace [*] ; and Hellas had been freed of all the anguish

sections.label: Section 2.305
sections.original: μόχθους οὓς ἀμφὶ Τροίαν
sections.translation: she suffered during [*] those ten long years her sons went wandering , spear in hand , around the walls of Troy ; brides had never been left desolate , nor hoary fathers childless .

sections.label: Section 309
sections.original: ἥκω λαβὼν σὸν παῖδ ’, ὃν εἰς ἄλλους δόμους
sections.translation: Behold I bring thy son with me , whom thou didst steal away to a neighbour ’ s house

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: λάθρᾳ θυγατρὸς τῆς ἐμῆς ὑπεξέθου .
sections.translation: without my daughter ’ s knowledge . Thou wert so sure this image of the goddess would protect thee and those who hid him , but thou hast not proved clever enough for Menelaus . And so if thou refuse to leave thy station here ,

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: ὅδ ’ ἀντὶ τοῦ σοῦ σώματος σφαγήσεται .
sections.translation: he shall be slain instead of thee . Wherefore weigh it well : wilt die thyself , or see him slain for the sin whereof thou art guilty against me and my daughter ?

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: ὦ δόξα δόξα , μυρίοισι δὴ βροτῶν
sections.translation: O fame , fame ! full many a man ere

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: οὐδὲν γεγῶσι βίοτον ὤγκωσας μέγαν .
sections.translation: now of no account hast thou to high estate exalted . Those , indeed , who truly have a fair repute , I count blest ; but those who get it by false pretences , I will never allow have aught but the accidental appearance of wisdom . Thou for instance , caitiff that thou art , didst thou ever wrest

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: Τροίαν ἀφείλου Πρίαμον , ὧδε φαῦλος ὤν ;
sections.translation: Troy from Priam with thy picked troops of Hellenes ? thou that hast raised such a storm , at the word of thy daughter , a mere child , and hast entered the lists with a poor captive ; unworthy I count thee of Troy ’ s capture , and Troy still more disgraced by thy victory .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: ἔξωθέν εἰσιν οἱ δοκοῦντες εὖ φρονεῖν
sections.translation: Those who only in appearance are men of sense make an outward show , but inwardly resemble the common herd , save it be in wealth , which is their chiefest strength . [*]

sections.label: Section 333
sections.original: Μενέλαε , φέρε δὴ διαπεράνωμεν λόγους ·
sections.translation: Come now , Menelaus , let us discuss this argument . Suppose I am slain [*] by thy daughter , and she work her will on me ,

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: μιαιφόνον μὲν οὐκέτ ’ ἂν φύγοι μύσος .
sections.translation: yet can she never escape the pollution of murder , and public opinion will make thee too an accomplice in this deed of blood , for thy share in the business must needs implicate thee . But even supposing I escape death myself , will ye kill my child ? Even then , how will his father
